  count(final_type, final_type_en, support_label, name = "n") %>%
  tidyr::complete(
    final_type = factor(final_type_levels_cn, levels = final_type_levels_cn),
    final_type_en = factor(final_type_levels_en, levels = final_type_levels_en),
    support_label = c("supported", "absent"),
    fill = list(n = 0)
  ) %>%
  group_by(final_type, final_type_en) %>%
  mutate(group_n = sum(n), proportion = if_else(group_n > 0, n / group_n, NA_real_)) %>%
  ungroup()

fig5B_stats <- {
  d <- fig5B_data %>% filter(!is.na(network_core_support_any))
  tab <- table(d$final_type_en, d$network_core_support_any)

  if (nrow(tab) < 2 || ncol(tab) < 2 || sum(tab) == 0) {
    tibble(
      method = "not_testable",
      statistic = NA_real_,
      p_value = NA_real_,
      df = NA_real_,
      note = "Insufficient non-missing data to perform Chi-square/Fisher"
    )
  } else {
    suppressWarnings(chisq_try <- tryCatch(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL))
    if (!is.null(chisq_try) && all(chisq_try$expected >= 5)) {
      tibble(
        method = "Chi-square",
        statistic = unname(chisq_try$statistic),
        p_value = chisq_try$p.value,
        df = unname(chisq_try$parameter),
        note = "Chi-square assumptions satisfied"
      )
    } else {
      fisher_try <- tryCatch(fisher.test(tab), error = function(e) e)
      if (inherits(fisher_try, "error")) {
        tibble(
          method = "fisher_failed",
          statistic = NA_real_,
          p_value = NA_real_,
          df = NA_real_,
          note = paste("Fisher test failed:", fisher_try$message)
        )
      } else {
        tibble(
          method = "Fisher",
          statistic = NA_real_,
          p_value = fisher_try$p.value,
          df = NA_real_,
          note = "Switched to Fisher due to expected count rule"
        )
      }
    }
  }
}

# ------------------------------
# 9) Generic helper for 5C / 5D
# ------------------------------
run_nonparam_panel <- function(df_in, value_col, panel_prefix, panel_title, y_label) {
  stopifnot(all(c("sample_id", "final_type", "final_type_en", value_col) %in% names(df_in)))

  plot_data <- df_in %>%
    transmute(
      sample_id,
      final_type,
      final_type_en,
      value = .data[[value_col]]
    )

  d_nonmiss <- plot_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    mutate(
      final_type = factor(as.character(final_type), levels = final_type_levels_cn),
      final_type_en = factor(as.character(final_type_en), levels = final_type_levels_en)
    )

  if (nrow(d_nonmiss) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf("%s has zero non-missing values after preprocessing.", panel_prefix), call. = FALSE)
  }

  group_n <- tibble(
    final_type = factor(final_type_levels_cn, levels = final_type_levels_cn),
    final_type_en = factor(final_type_levels_en, levels = final_type_levels_en)
  ) %>%
    left_join(d_nonmiss %>% count(final_type, final_type_en, name = "n"),
              by = c("final_type", "final_type_en")) %>%
    mutate(n = replace_na(n, 0L))

  kw_tbl <- if (nrow(d_nonmiss) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(d_nonmiss$final_type_en[!is.na(d_nonmiss$final_type_en)]) >= 2) {
    kw <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(value ~ final_type_en, data = d_nonmiss), error = function(e) e)
    if (inherits(kw, "error")) {
      tibble(method = "kruskal.test", statistic = NA_real_, df = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_, note = kw$message)
    } else {
      tibble(method = "kruskal.test", statistic = unname(kw$statistic), df = unname(kw$parameter), p_value = kw$p.value, note = "OK")
    }
  } else {
    tibble(method = "kruskal.test", statistic = NA_real_, df = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_, note = "Insufficient non-missing samples or groups")
  }

  dunn_tbl <- if (nrow(d_nonmiss) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(d_nonmiss$final_type_en[!is.na(d_nonmiss$final_type_en)]) >= 2) {
    dunn <- tryCatch(FSA::dunnTest(value ~ final_type_en, data = d_nonmiss, method = "bh"), error = function(e) e)
    if (inherits(dunn, "error")) {
      tibble(comparison = NA_character_, z = NA_real_, p_unadjusted = NA_real_, p_adjusted_BH = NA_real_, note = dunn$message)
    } else {
      as_tibble(dunn$res) %>%
        transmute(
          comparison = Comparison,
          z = Z,
          p_unadjusted = P.unadj,
          p_adjusted_BH = P.adj,
          note = "OK"
        )
    }
  } else {
    tibble(comparison = NA_character_, z = NA_real_, p_unadjusted = NA_real_, p_adjusted_BH = NA_real_, note = "Insufficient non-missing samples or groups")
  }

  effect_tbl <- if (nrow(d_nonmiss) >= 3 && dplyr::n_distinct(d_nonmiss$final_type_en[!is.na(d_nonmiss$final_type_en)]) >= 2) {
    eff <- tryCatch(rstatix::kruskal_effsize(d_nonmiss, value ~ final_type_en), error = function(e) e)
    if (inherits(eff, "error")) {
      tibble(method = "epsilon_squared", effect_size = NA_real_, magnitude = NA_character_, note = eff$message)
    } else {
      eff %>%
        transmute(
          method = "epsilon_squared",
          effect_size = effsize,
          magnitude = magnitude,
          note = "OK"
        )
    }
  } else {
    tibble(method = "epsilon_squared", effect_size = NA_real_, magnitude = NA_character_, note = "Insufficient non-missing samples or groups")
  }

  p <- ggplot(d_nonmiss, aes(x = final_type_en, y = value, fill = final_type_en)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.65, alpha = 0.85, color = "#4D4D4D") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.16, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.70, color = "#333333") +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = final_type_colors_en, guide = "none", drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = panel_title,
      x = "Sample state",
      y = y_label
    ) +
    theme_fig

  save_pdf_out(p, paste0(panel_prefix, ".pdf"), paste0(panel_prefix, "_plot"), width = 6.5, height = 5)

  audit_plot <- plot_data %>%
    transmute(
      section = "plot_data",
      sample_id,
      final_type = as.character(final_type),
      final_type_en = as.character(final_type_en),
      value,
      stat_name = value_col,
      stat_value = as.character(value),
      note = "Sample-level plotting data"
    )

  audit_group <- group_n %>%
    transmute(
      section = "group_n",
      sample_id = NA_character_,
      final_type = as.character(final_type),
      final_type_en = as.character(final_type_en),
      value = as.numeric(n),
      stat_name = "group_n",
      stat_value = as.character(n),
      note = "Group sizes"
    )

  audit_kw <- kw_tbl %>%
    transmute(
      section = "kw",
      sample_id = NA_character_,
      final_type = NA_character_,
      final_type_en = NA_character_,
      value = p_value,
      stat_name = method,
      stat_value = paste0("statistic=", statistic, "; df=", df, "; p=", p_value),
      note
    )

  audit_dunn <- dunn_tbl %>%
    transmute(
      section = "dunn",
      sample_id = NA_character_,
      final_type = NA_character_,
      final_type_en = comparison,
      value = p_adjusted_BH,
      stat_name = "Dunn_BH",
      stat_value = paste0("z=", z, "; p_unadj=", p_unadjusted, "; p_adj=", p_adjusted_BH),
      note
    )

  audit_eff <- effect_tbl %>%
    transmute(
      section = "effect_size",
      sample_id = NA_character_,
      final_type = NA_character_,
      final_type_en = magnitude,
      value = effect_size,
      stat_name = method,
      stat_value = as.character(effect_size),
      note
    )

  write_panel_audit(
    list(audit_plot, audit_group, audit_kw, audit_dunn, audit_eff),
    paste0(panel_prefix, "_audit.tsv"),
    paste0(panel_prefix, "_audit")
  )

  return(p)
}

fig5C_input <- plot_base %>%

    final_type_en,
    network_core_support_n
  )

p5C <- run_nonparam_panel(
  df_in = fig5C_input,
  value_col = "network_core_support_n",
  panel_prefix = "Fig5C",
  panel_title = "Core network support strength across sample states",
  y_label = "Number of core network supports"
)

# ------------------------------
# 11) Figure 5D
# ------------------------------
fig5D_input <- plot_base %>%
  transmute(
    sample_id,
    final_type,
    final_type_en,
    network_marker_specific_burden
  )

p5D <- run_nonparam_panel(
  df_in = fig5D_input,
  value_col = "network_marker_specific_burden",

  panel_title = "Marker-specific network burden across sample states",

)

# ------------------------------
# 12) Combined Figure 5 (4 panels)

combined_plot <- (p5A | p5B) / (p5C | p5D) +

save_pdf_out(combined_plot, "Fig5_combined.pdf", "Fig5_combined_plot", width = 13, height = 10)

审计并解读结果

Thought for 32s


这次结果比前一版明显更好：Figure 5 现在已经收束成一个干净的 4-panel 总图，A 是 network schematic，B 是 core support 比例，C 是 core support 强度，D 是 marker-specific burden；此前最明显的“空白稳定性 panel”问题已经被移除，所以图面结构上比上一轮更清楚，也更容易被审稿人读懂。运行环境记录也完整，R 4.4.2、ggplot2 4.0.2、patchwork 1.3.2、ggraph 2.2.2 等都已载入，复现性层面没有明显短板。


但从“是否已经足够作为主文最终版”的角度看，我的判断是：还差最后一个关键点——panel C 仍然没有彻底站住。 也就是说，现在这版 Figure 5 已经从“版式不稳”进化到了“论证重心更清楚”，但还没有完全进入“可以放心定稿”的状态。


具体来看，A panel 是合格的。
它把 network addon 的对象讲清楚了：图中有 g__Angelica、g__Carthamus、g__Citrus、g__Paeonia、g__Prunus 五个节点，同时区分了 reproducible/core-like 边和 marker-specific 边。对于 Figure 5 这种本来就定位为“addon evidence”的图来说，A 已经完成了它最重要的任务：解释 network addon 看见的是什么结构，而不是去改写 final_type。 这张图现在可以留。


B panel 仍然是一个弱结果，但它现在是“诚实的弱结果”。
图上三组柱子几乎全部是灰色 100%，说明 binary 的 core support 几乎不区分三类状态。换句话说，当前 network_core_support_any 在样本层面基本没有提供分组鉴别力。这个结果不是脚本错，更像是网络 addon 在当前定义下给出的真实结论：core support 在这里是稀缺或近乎恒定事件。 所以 B 可以保留，但它不适合作为 Figure 5 的主力证据。


C panel 依然是当前整张图的主要风险点。
它的标题和 y 轴都在说 “Number of core network supports”，但图上仍然出现大约 -0.4 到 0.4 的负值和小数。这使它在语义上很难被解释成“数量”或“个数”。也就是说，即便从图面上看 Unresolved 似乎更分散，这个结论也不能直接被写成“未定型样本拥有更高或更低的 core support 数量”，因为变量本身的定义还不自洽。当前 panel C 更像某种中心化或标准化后的连续 score，而不是 count。只要这一点不改，C 就仍然不够稳。


D panel 现在是整张图里最有解释价值的一张 quantitative 图。
从可视层面看，Unresolved 组的散点范围更宽，离散度也更大，而另外两组更集中。这说明 marker-specific burden 更可能在反映未定型样本的结构异质性，而不是直接替稳定多谱系型背书。换句话说，network addon 当前最强的贡献，不是“证明 stable multi-lineage 更强”，而是“解释 unresolved 为什么更难收束、为什么结构上更杂”。这其实是一个可以写进讨论部分的有价值方向。


所以，如果把这张图翻译成一句论文式结论，我会建议你这样理解：


Figure 5 currently supports a heterogeneity-oriented interpretation rather than a class-separation interpretation.
也就是：network addon 更擅长补充说明未定型样本的结构异质性，而不是进一步把三类状态在 network 维度上干净地区分开。


更具体地说，现在这张图最安全、最能站住的叙事是：

Network add-on analysis primarily provided structural context after state assignment. The most interpretable quantitative signal was the broader dispersion of marker-specific network burden in unresolved samples, consistent with greater structural heterogeneity in samples that fail to converge to a clean state call. In contrast, binary core-support status appeared largely invariant across states, and the current core-support “number” panel still requires semantic clarification before it can be treated as count-based evidence.

按“审稿人会不会质疑”的角度排优先级，我会这样给你结论：


可以保留且基本合格：A、D

可以保留但只能弱写：B

仍需最后修正或改名重释：C


因此，我对这次结果的最终判断是：


Figure 5 现在已经可以作为“接近定稿的版本”进入正文讨论，但前提是你不要把 panel C 当成 count 来写。
如果你愿意保留 C，就应该把它重新定义为一个连续型 support score，并同步改标题与 y 轴；如果你坚持把它写成 “number of supports”，那它还需要再修一次。除此之外，这一版已经比前面所有版本都更接近可用主文图。
